comment_text.sn16.10:1.4: Ānanda’s use of “sir” (bhante) here suggests this took place after the Buddha’s passing (DN 16:6.2.3), which is normally the case when the Buddha is not mentioned in the introduction.
comment_text.sn16.10:1.5: A certain tension arose between the strict Mahākassapa and the more open Ānanda after the Buddha’s passing. At the First Council, Ānanda was repeatedly admonished by senior monks apparently siding with Kassapa (Kd 21:1.10.2).
comment_text.sn16.10:3.2: Ānanda was from Sakya, not Videha, a problem that evidently vexed the commentators. The Pali commentaries explains vedeha as “learned”, or say his mother was from Videha. The Mahāvastu, on the other hand, says that after the Buddha went forth, Ānanda went to Videha and undertook a vow of silence (maunavrata, Mvu 83; Senart 3.176). The Mūlasarvastivāda Vinaya says rather that it was on Mount Videha that the Buddha entrusted Ānanda with the design for the robes (Mu Kd 7; GBM 6.814). None of these are cogent. The point is that Thullatissā was such an idiot, she didn’t even know where Ānanda came from.
comment_text.sn16.10:4.5: For “lady” (mātugāma), see note on SN 37.3:1.1.
comment_text.sn16.10:4.6: Apparently he is referring to the admonition of Ānanda at the First Council.
comment_text.sn16.10:6.4: This note referring to the previous Sutta is in the Pali text.
comment_text.sn16.10:9.1: Text has sattaratana, seven “cubits”, i.e. the length from elbow to fingertips, or about 45cm.

root_text.sn16.10:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahākassapo sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn16.10:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yenāyasmā mahākassapo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn16.10:1.4: “āyāma, bhante kassapa, yena aññataro bhikkhunupassayo tenupasaṅkamissāmā”ti.
root_text.sn16.10:1.5: “Gaccha tvaṁ, āvuso ānanda, bahukicco tvaṁ bahukaraṇīyo”ti.
root_text.sn16.10:1.6: Dutiyampi kho āyasmā ānando āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn16.10:1.7: “āyāma, bhante kassapa, yena aññataro bhikkhunupassayo tenupasaṅkamissāmā”ti.
root_text.sn16.10:1.8: “Gaccha tvaṁ, āvuso ānanda, bahukicco tvaṁ bahukaraṇīyo”ti.
root_text.sn16.10:1.9: Tatiyampi kho āyasmā ānando āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn16.10:1.10: “āyāma, bhante kassapa, yena aññataro bhikkhunupassayo tenupasaṅkamissāmā”ti.
root_text.sn16.10:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya āyasmatā ānandena pacchāsamaṇena yena aññataro bhikkhunupassayo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.sn16.10:2.2: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhuniyo yenāyasmā mahākassapo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.sn16.10:2.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho tā bhikkhuniyo āyasmā mahākassapo dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi.
root_text.sn16.10:2.4: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo tā bhikkhuniyo dhammiyā kathāya sandassetvā samādapetvā samuttejetvā sampahaṁsetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi.
root_text.sn16.10:3.1: Atha kho thullatissā bhikkhunī anattamanā anattamanavācaṁ nicchāresi:
root_text.sn16.10:3.2: “kiṁ pana ayyo mahākassapo, ayyassa ānandassa vedehamunino sammukhā dhammaṁ bhāsitabbaṁ maññati?
root_text.sn16.10:3.3: Seyyathāpi nāma sūcivāṇijako sūcikārassa santike sūciṁ vikketabbaṁ maññeyya;
root_text.sn16.10:3.4: evameva ayyo mahākassapo ayyassa ānandassa vedehamunino sammukhā dhammaṁ bhāsitabbaṁ maññatī”ti.
root_text.sn16.10:4.1: Assosi kho āyasmā mahākassapo thullatissāya bhikkhuniyā imaṁ vācaṁ bhāsamānāya.
root_text.sn16.10:4.2: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn16.10:4.3: “kiṁ nu kho, āvuso ānanda, ahaṁ sūcivāṇijako, tvaṁ sūcikāro;
root_text.sn16.10:4.6: “Āgamehi tvaṁ, āvuso ānanda, mā te saṅgho uttari upaparikkhi.
root_text.sn16.10:5.2: api nu tvaṁ bhagavato sammukhā bhikkhusaṅghe upanīto:
root_text.sn16.10:5.3: ‘ahaṁ, bhikkhave, yāvade ākaṅkhāmi vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.sn16.10:5.4: Ānandopi, bhikkhave, yāvade ākaṅkhati vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharatī’”ti?
root_text.sn16.10:6.1: “Ahaṁ kho, āvuso, bhagavato sammukhā bhikkhusaṅghe upanīto:
root_text.sn16.10:6.2: ‘ahaṁ, bhikkhave, yāvade ākaṅkhāmi vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.sn16.10:6.3: Kassapopi, bhikkhave, yāvade ākaṅkhati vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi …pe… paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharatī’ti …pe….
root_text.sn16.10:6.4: (Navannaṁ anupubbavihārasamāpattīnaṁ pañcannañca abhiññānaṁ evaṁ vitthāro veditabbo.)
root_text.sn16.10:7.2: api nu tvaṁ bhagavato sammukhā bhikkhusaṅghe upanīto:
root_text.sn16.10:7.3: ‘ahaṁ, bhikkhave, āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.sn16.10:7.4: Ānandopi, bhikkhave, āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharatī’”ti?
root_text.sn16.10:8.1: “Ahaṁ kho, āvuso, bhagavato sammukhā bhikkhusaṅghe upanīto:
root_text.sn16.10:8.2: ‘ahaṁ, bhikkhave, āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.sn16.10:8.3: Kassapopi, bhikkhave, āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharatī’ti.
root_text.sn16.10:9.1: Sattaratanaṁ vā, āvuso, nāgaṁ aḍḍhaṭṭhamaratanaṁ vā tālapattikāya chādetabbaṁ maññeyya, yo me cha abhiññā chādetabbaṁ maññeyyā”ti.
root_text.sn16.10:10.1: Cavittha ca pana thullatissā bhikkhunī brahmacariyamhāti.

translation_text.sn16.10:1.2: At one time Venerable Mahākassapa was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn16.10:1.3: Then Venerable Ānanda robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to Mahākassapa and said,
translation_text.sn16.10:1.4: “Kassapa, come, sir. Let’s go to one of the nuns’ quarters.”
translation_text.sn16.10:1.5: “You go, Reverend Ānanda. You’re the one with many duties and responsibilities.”
translation_text.sn16.10:1.9: And a third time, Ānanda said,
translation_text.sn16.10:1.10: “Come, Honorable Kassapa. Let’s go to one of the nuns’ quarters.”
translation_text.sn16.10:2.1: Then Venerable Mahākassapa robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went with Venerable Ānanda as his second monk to one of the nuns’ quarters, where he sat on the seat spread out.
translation_text.sn16.10:2.2: And then several nuns went up to Mahākassapa, bowed, and sat down to one side.
translation_text.sn16.10:2.3: Mahākassapa educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired those nuns with a Dhamma talk,
translation_text.sn16.10:2.4: after which he got up from his seat and left.
translation_text.sn16.10:3.1: But the nun Thullatissā was upset and blurted out,
translation_text.sn16.10:3.2: “What is the venerable Mahākassapa thinking, that he’d teach Dhamma in front of the venerable Ānanda, the Videhan sage?
translation_text.sn16.10:3.3: He’s like a needle peddler who thinks they can sell a needle to a needle maker!”
translation_text.sn16.10:4.1: Mahākassapa heard Thullatissā say these words,
translation_text.sn16.10:4.3: “Is that right, Reverend Ānanda? Am I the needle peddler and you the needle maker?
translation_text.sn16.10:4.4: Or am I the needle maker and you the needle peddler?”
translation_text.sn16.10:4.5: “Forgive her, sir. The lady’s a fool.”
translation_text.sn16.10:4.6: “Hold on, Reverend Ānanda! Don’t make the Saṅgha investigate you further!
translation_text.sn16.10:5.1: What do you think, Reverend Ānanda?
translation_text.sn16.10:5.2: Was it you who the Buddha brought up before the Saṅgha of mendicants, saying:
translation_text.sn16.10:5.3: ‘Mendicants, whenever I want, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, I enter and remain in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.sn16.10:6.1: “I was the one the Buddha brought up before the Saṅgha of mendicants, saying:
translation_text.sn16.10:6.2: ‘Mendicants, whenever I want, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, I enter and remain in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.sn16.10:6.4: (The nine progressive meditations and the five insights should be told in full.)
translation_text.sn16.10:7.1: What do you think, Reverend Ānanda?
translation_text.sn16.10:7.2: Was it you who the Buddha brought up before the Saṅgha of mendicants, saying:
translation_text.sn16.10:7.3: ‘I have realized the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And I live having realized it with my own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.sn16.10:8.1: “I was the one the Buddha brought up before the Saṅgha of mendicants, saying:
translation_text.sn16.10:8.2: ‘I have realized the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And I live having realized it with my own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.sn16.10:9.1: Reverend, you might as well think to hide a bull elephant that’s three or three and a half meters tall behind a palm leaf as to hide my six insights.”
translation_text.sn16.10:10.1: But the nun Thullatissā fell from the spiritual life.
